data.title: Pokémon cards ready for 'Polymarket moment' with trading potentially moving to blockchain soon after decades of physical transactions.
data.description: Trading of Pokémon cards is on the brink of experiencing a significant change similar to the "Polymarket moment." For years, the trading process has involved in-person meetups and physical shipments. However, there is now a shift towards on-chain trading, marking a significant transition in the industry.

data.title: Roche's HER2 (4B5) test gets CE IVDR approval to identify patients with HER2-ultralow breast cancer.
data.description: The VENTANA HER2 (4B5) test has received CE-IVDR approval, allowing it to assist in determining HER2-ultralow status for identifying metastatic breast cancer patients suitable for ENHERTU treatment. Additionally, the test is now authorized to aid in identifying patients with specific requirements for...

data.title: Idorsia partners with Stanford & Duke for IMPACT-HTN initiative to improve care for patients with difficult-to-control hypertension. New program led by Drs. Bhalla and Vemulapalli aims to standardize care and utilize AI tech to identify patients who may benefit from innovative therapies including Idorsia's TRYVIO™.
data.description: On September 5, 2025, Idorsia Ltd (SIX: IDIA) revealed a groundbreaking collaboration with the Stanford Hypertension Center and Duke Heart Center in Allschwil, Switzerland, and Radnor, Philadelphia, respectively. This partnership has led to the introduction of IMPACT-HTN, a cutting-edge three-phase program aimed at revamping the management of difficult-to-control hypertension. Patients grappling with this condition, necessitating numerous medications, encounter mounting difficulties in coordinating care, investigating root causes, and experiencing deteriorating outcomes. Spearheaded by Dr. Vivek Bhalla from Stanford University School of Medicine and Dr. Sreekanth Vemulapalli from Duke University School of Medicine, this initiative anticipates the production of real-world evidence, establishment of standardized clinical protocols, and provision of scalable AI-driven tools. These technological advancements will aid in identifying individuals with challenging hypertension who could benefit from novel therapies that exploit fresh pathways, such as Idorsia's innovative once-daily treatment, TRYVIO™ (aprocitentan). This medication marks the first systemic hypertension treatment to target a new pathway in more than three decades.

data.title: Kay Properties hosts Investor Conference with 150 investors for passive real estate insights on DSTs, 1031, and 721 UPREIT strategies.
data.description: In Torrance, California, Kay Properties organized its Annual Kay Investor Day Conference, bringing together almost 150 accredited investors from various parts of the country. The event aimed to provide valuable information on passive real estate investing, focusing on Delaware Statutory Trusts (DSTs), 1031 exchanges, 721 UPREIT strategies, and estate planning. The conference included expert panels, sponsor presentations, and interactive workshops to educate attendees.

data.title: Broadcom beats Q3 earnings estimate, reports $15.95 billion revenue, $1.69 earnings per share, up 22% from the previous year.
data.description: Broadcom Inc (NASDAQ: AVGO) released their Q3 financial results, exceeding analyst expectations. Their revenue for the quarter was $15.95 billion, higher than the estimated $15.83 billion. Adjusted earnings stood at $1.69 per share, surpassing the anticipated $1.65 per share. The company saw a 22% increase in total revenue compared to the previous year, fueled by growth in custom AI accelerators, networking, and VMware. In terms of cash flow, Broadcom generated around $7.17 billion from operations during this period. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
